target_capability: Research Palantir's full methodology, compare it with adjacent academic and industry work, and extract a usable Yuanli OS adoption model.

source_hierarchy.label: source_of_truth
source_hierarchy.rule: Use official Palantir docs and investor materials for Palantir product and delivery claims.

source_hierarchy.label: standards_and_peer_review
source_hierarchy.rule: Use standards bodies and peer-reviewed papers to anchor enterprise ontology, knowledge graph, digital twin, and decision-support concepts.

source_hierarchy.label: industry_research
source_hierarchy.rule: Use McKinsey, Gartner, and comparable public research to assess adoption patterns and enterprise implications.

source_hierarchy.label: inference
source_hierarchy.rule: Use inference only to connect Palantir's stack to Yuanli OS design decisions, and label it explicitly.

remaining_unknowns: Palantir's deepest internal implementation details remain proprietary.
Paid analyst reports were not used as core evidence.
No claim is made that Palantir invented every underlying concept; the comparison focuses on engineering synthesis and delivery method.

sources.title: Platform Overview
sources.notes: Primary source for Foundry platform shape and operating model.

sources.title: Ontology Overview
sources.notes: Primary source for object, link, action, and operational ontology claims.

sources.title: Why create an Ontology?
sources.notes: Best source for the business rationale behind the ontology layer.

sources.title: AIP Features
sources.notes: Primary source for AI functions, agents, semantic grounding, and governance patterns.

sources.title: Architecture Center Overview
sources.notes: Primary source for deployment, architecture, and operational hardening.

sources.title: Q3 2023 Business Update
sources.notes: Primary source for AIP bootcamp framing and time-to-value claims.

sources.title: Reports 2024
sources.notes: Used to anchor reported business updates and official communications.

sources.title: Events
sources.notes: Used to confirm latest public reporting cadence.

sources.title: ORG Ontology
sources.notes: Represents a standards-based view of organizational entities, roles, and reporting lines.

sources.title: Knowledge Graphs, Large Language Models and Large Multimodal Models: A Survey
sources.notes: Useful to separate semantic grounding from raw LLM behavior.

sources.title: Large Language Models and Knowledge Graphs: Opportunities and Challenges
sources.notes: Used for the bridge between symbolic structure and generative systems.

sources.title: Digital Twin: State-of-the-Art Future Challenge and Research Directions
sources.notes: Anchor source for digital twin as model plus data plus feedback loop.

sources.title: A Cognitive Digital Twin for Intelligent Decision-Making
sources.notes: Useful for the move from passive twin to decision-support twin.

sources.title: Gartner Identifies Top Trends in Data and Analytics for 2022
sources.notes: Used as the clearest public Gartner statement on decision intelligence.

sources.title: Why Agents Are the Next Frontier of Generative AI
sources.notes: Useful for the enterprise framing of agents beyond chatbot use cases.

sources.title: Azure Digital Twins Overview
sources.notes: Used to compare a mainstream digital twin implementation model with Palantir's broader operational layer.

sources.title: OWL 2 Web Ontology Language Document Overview
sources.notes: Useful for separating formal ontology tooling from Palantir's productized operational ontology.

sources.title: RDF 1.1 Concepts and Abstract Syntax
sources.notes: Anchor source for graph data modeling fundamentals.

sources.title: Shapes Constraint Language (SHACL)
sources.notes: Useful for understanding graph constraints and validation, which Palantir handles in a more product-specific way.

sources.title: SPARQL 1.1 Overview
sources.notes: Useful as a contrast case: semantic query power alone is not the same as operational execution.
